steps.text: Review and consult sampling method guidance documents and procedures to determine the level of validation associated with the method.
Indicate whether the method uses a physical sampling or collection tool/technique, or whether the method is a field measurement process.
Specify conditions for either type of method.
Prepare a sampling validation plan that encompasses all aspects of the validation activity and follows a standardized format.
Document and either limit the variables tested or ensure that a range of conditions are included in the test.
Document the process by which the samples are collected.
Define the matrix being sampled and the purpose of the sampling effort.
Define all aspects of the equipment/instrument's set-up, calibration, and operation, as well as sample preparation, sample cleanup, and appropriate Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) checks.
Clearly define the purpose of a sampling method and the intended use of the data.
Define and clearly describe method scope and applicability.
Identify and address interferences during the development of the sampling validation plan.
Evaluate selectivity by sampling an appropriate environmental media both with and without the expected interferences.
Address chemical sampling devices versus physical sampling devices when evaluating sampling method selectivity.
Select sampling equipment and methods considering material compatibility, chemical compatibility, sample volume capabilities, physical requirements, ease of operation, and decontamination.
Calibrate, perform routine maintenance, and standardize or certify (at least annually) all instruments used to measure or analyze samples in the field.
Certify and/or calibrate equipment used to obtain sample volumetric and/or weight measurements using prescriptive or performance-based specifications.
Estimate method bias by measuring reference materials, comparing results to an alternate method, or analyzing spiked materials.
Define the term 'detection limit' and provide a description of how it was evaluated during method validation.
Verify limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models by testing materials containing the analyte at the claimed detection level.
Address detection limit as it applies to instrument detection capability.
Conduct ruggedness testing using experimental designs to examine method performance under minor variations in operating or environmental conditions.
Establish and verify the validity of the sampling method by conducting field studies.
Prepare a sampling method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.
Provide documentation of successful completion of field studies in the sampling method validation report.
Provide an overall summary of the validation report.
Provide background information on sampling method development and state the purpose of the method in the introduction.
Describe the sampling method validation technique/experimental design, including a description of the test method/procedure and details of equipment/locations used with calibration status.

Discuss how the sampling method scope and applicability served to define the range of method performance, addressing relevant topic areas such as process components, analytes, matrix, analyte levels, advantages/limitations, equipment, and data quality objectives.
Include a discussion of how selectivity was evaluated through the sampling of appropriate environmental media both with and without expected interferences.
Include a statement describing the decision of how in-depth uncertainty was determined and what planning took place in advance of samples being collected in the field.

Include a statement focusing on the equipment that is used to measure or analyze samples in the field and how the required calibration, routine maintenance, and annual standardization are accomplished.

Include a discussion of how systematic errors were accounted for in the sampling plan through measurements of reasonably well known composition material, results comparisons to alternative methods or procedures, or using analyzed spikes.
Include a discussion of sampling method performance after minor changes are experienced in operating or environmental conditions.
Include a discussion of preserving the collected samples’ physical and chemical properties and security from the collection site to the analytical laboratory.

Include a discussion of how the sampling method determined the appropriate quantity of sample to be taken to support analysis, how the detection limit was defined and evaluated, and how limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models were verified.
Include a discussion of the number or items or the quantity constituting a sample, as appropriate for the method, whether the samples are composites or grab.
Address safety concerns and procedures, including personal protective equipment, first aid, physical hazards, and chemical hazards.
Provide a discussion of the defined quality control/quality assurance (QA/QC) checks and guidances.
Discuss method development.
Discuss results of the validation testing and an evaluation including comparison with the reference substances and preparations, acceptance criteria and recommendations.
Note any changes made to the sampling method as a result of the validation studies.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.
Include the sampling validation plan as a section within the plan or as a stand-alone document attached as an appendix.
Integrate the contributions and requirements of all stakeholders and present this information in a clear, concise format.
Conduct validation planning as part of initial planning (e.g., directed planning process).
Communicate the information and documentation identified in the validation plans to the laboratory as part of the Statement of Work (SOW).

Peer review the sampling method for chemical and radiochemical parameters, either in accordance with the EPA Science Technology Policy Council’s Peer Review Handbook or via a documented alternate procedure.

preconditions.text: Ensure that the new collection method is consistent with the planned analytical methods to be utilized.
Field measurement processes are to be performed.
Prior to publication of the sampling method.

constraints.text: Any determination of uncertainty or bias of an environmental measurement must be made in relation to both the analytical method and the sampling method for chemical and radiochemical parameters.
Control and document variability to determine the range for purposeful uses and identify techniques to be utilized when using the sampling technology.
Address safety concerns and procedures in both the sampling method and the validation plan, including personal protective equipment and first aid.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
The choice of sampling equipment can be crucial to the task of collecting a sample appropriate for the intended use.
Calibration must follow procedures in the manufacturer's users' manual, laboratory procedures, or as defined by the method.
Calibration procedures must ensure appropriate method performance characteristics by the instrument at the time of analysis.
Typical detection limits claimed by the manufacturer are not always achievable in the field due to environmental conditions, sample type, etc.
The validation report must, at a minimum, address the information contained in Table 1.

The validation report must provide background information on sampling method development, a description of actual validation techniques, changes made to the method, and recommendations for future work.
Safety concerns include personal protective equipment, first aid, physical hazards, and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.
For emergency response situations, follow the emergency response method validation policy (Agency Policy Directive Number FEM-2010-01).
